'Ndrina Cordì.description: The Cordì are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Locri, Calabria, Italy. They are historic rivals of the Cataldo clan and have a strong presence in Portigliola, Gerace, and Antonimina. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking and a long-running feud with the Cataldo family, which has caused over 30 deaths since the 1990s. The clan remains active, with members arrested as recently as 2006.

descriptions: Cataldo and Cordi were in conflict in the Locri feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Locri : ebbe inizio nel 1967 a causa dell'omicidio di Domenico Cordì compiuto per punire uno sgarro verso Antonio Macrì . Da allora le cosche Cataldo e Cordi si sono combattute a più riprese

descriptions: The Cataldo are historical rivals of the Cordì.
edges.evidence_quote: storici rivali dei Cordì

Cártel de Colima.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and methamphetamine production, founded in 1988 by Jesús and Adán Amezcua. Originally a cocaine trafficking group for Colombian cartels, it later specialized in methamphetamine. The cartel was dismantled in 2005 and some members joined the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of.description: Far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the United States during the 1970s and early 1980s. Developed from a Baptist congregation founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri, and later operated a compound in northern Arkansas. Dissolved after a 1985 siege and arrest of its leaders.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The CSA had links to the Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA had links to other radical organizations, including the Aryan Brotherhood, the Mountain Church, and The Order

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: CSA leader Ellison had close ties to Aryan Nations.
edges.evidence_quote: Ellison had close ties to the Ku Klux Klan and Aryan Nations

edges.evidence_quote: the Pagans Motorcycle Club provided the CSA with training in booby trap devices and survival techniques in return for weapons and ammunition.

descriptions: The CSA evolved from and succeeded the Zarephath-Horeb Community Church.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA developed from a Baptist congregation, the Zarephath-Horeb Community Church, which was founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri. Over time, Zarephath-Horeb evolved into an extremist militant group and it was rechristened the CSA.

Black Guerrilla Family.description: African American prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison in California. It is a Marxist-Leninist revolutionary group promoting black power and prisoner dignity, involved in drug trafficking, burglary, and homicide. The BGF remains active with estimated 100-300 members and thousands of associates.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood struck against Black Guerrilla Family, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They decided to strike against the African-Americans who were forming their own militant group called the Black Guerrilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood engaged in a race war with Black Guerrilla Family in 1975.
edges.evidence_quote: The Aryan Brotherhood grew quickly in the California prison system and eventually engaged in a race war in 1975 with the other prison gangs such as La Nuestra Familia, and Black Guerilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood was formed in response to Black Guerilla Family.
edges.evidence_quote: Aryan Brotherhood had its beginnings in the San Quentin State Prison in 1964 as a response to the newly formed black prison gang Black Guerilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood attacked Black Guerrilla Family members.
edges.evidence_quote: Decidieron atacar a los afroamericanos que estaban formando su propio grupo militante llamado Black Guerrilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood was at war with Black Guerrilla Family in 1975.
edges.evidence_quote: eventualmente comenzó una guerra racial en 1975 con las otras pandillas de la prisión como ... la Black Guerilla Family

Artistas Asesinos.description: Mexican criminal organization founded in 2008 by Jorge Ernesto Sáenz and Éder Ángel Martínez, originally serving as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ciudad Juárez and the El Paso metropolitan area. Known for their artistic skills in drawing and graffiti, they engage in homicide, drug dealing, extortion, and robbery. Currently one of the main violent groups in Chihuahua, controlling part of the Cereso 3 prison and in conflict with rival groups.

Lucerne Street Doggz.description: Street gang based in the Lucerne Street area of Mattapan, Boston, founded in 2003. Primarily African American, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. In 2006, the gang was responsible for 37 shootings, accounting for 10% of all shootings in Boston that year.

Moccia clan.description: The Moccia clan is a powerful Camorra clan operating in the northeast area of the Metropolitan City of Naples, Italy, with a strong presence in Lazio. Founded in the 1960s by Gennaro Moccia, it is considered the oldest still active clan in the region. After Gennaro's death, his wife Anna Mazza led the clan, becoming the first woman convicted of Mafia-related crimes in Italy. The clan controls drug trafficking, money laundering, and political corruption, and is known for its vertical structure and lack of pentiti.

descriptions: Alfieri clan allied with Moccia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: He also made new alliances with the ... Moccia clan from Afragola

edges.evidence_quote: Stando a quanto dichiarato dal pentito di Nola Ciro Di Domenico, a tramare contro Guerriero furono tre clan (i Cava, i Russo e i Moccia )

descriptions: Members of Clan Cava, Clan Moccia, and Clan Russo were arrested together for usury and extortion, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: In data 17 giugno 2011 , in Mugnano del Cardinale , San Paolo Bel Sito ed Afragola , nell'ambito di un'attività di indagini finalizzata a contrastare il fenomeno dell'usura e delle estorsioni ai danni di imprenditori operanti nel settore dell’edilizia, militari del Nucleo investigativo, dopo aver acquisito gravi elementi di colpevolezza, in esecuzione di provvedimento emesso dall'Autorità Giudiziaria, sottoponevano a fermo di Polizia Giudiziaria 5 pregiudicati esponenti dei clan Cava di Quindici , Moccia di Afragola e Russo di Nola , ritenuti responsabili dei reati di usura ed estorsione aggravata dalla connotazione mafiosa, per aver commesso il fatto avvalendosi della forza di intimidazione dovuta all'appartenenza ai summenzionati sodalizi criminosi.

descriptions: Moccia clan is listed as an ally of Licciardi clan in the infobox.

descriptions: Clan Magliulo and Clan Moccia were in a bloody feud over a construction contract in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni novanta furono protagonisti di una faida sanguinosa contro il clan Moccia: le due famiglie si contendevano l'appalto per la costruzione dell'attuale Rione Salicelle.

descriptions: Magliulo clan killed Vincenzo Moccia, leading to a war that Moccia clan won.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, Mazza's favorite son Vincenzo Moccia, called Angioletto , was killed by the rival Magliulo clan, that led to another bloody war between the clans, the Moccias came out victorious.

descriptions: Clan Moccia replaced Clan Magliulo, indicating a structural succession.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha preso il posto del clan Magliulo , abbattendo di fatto uno dei pilastri del clan Alfieri .

descriptions: Clan Moccia had a feud with Clan Natale over drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: un grupo conocido como la Organización Herrera transporta múltiples toneladas de cocaína desde Sudamérica a Guatemala y Honduras en nombre del Cartel de Sinaloa.

Continentals.description: The Continentals were a Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown in the early 1960s. Founded in 1961 by American-born Chinese high school students to protect Chinese students from attacks by other ethnic groups. The gang engaged primarily in non-violent crimes such as vandalism and had up to 100 members. It was active only from 1961 to 1964, succeeded by more violent gangs like the Ghost Shadows and Flying Dragons.
